description: AI Town frontend + AgentBurg Python backend hybrid. Persistent world where AI agents live, trade, and compete with time compression (1 real hour = 1 agent day).

userStories.title: Fork AI Town and strip Convex dependency
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the AI Town frontend code (PixiJS 2D map, React UI) without Convex backend so I can connect it to our Python FastAPI backend.
acceptanceCriteria: AI Town frontend code copied into project (src/frontend/ or similar)
All Convex imports replaced with placeholder API client module
Frontend builds without errors (npm run build passes)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create API bridge: frontend REST client → FastAPI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the frontend to fetch world state and agent data from our Python FastAPI backend instead of Convex.
acceptanceCriteria: API client module calls GET /api/v1/world/status for world state
API client calls GET /api/v1/agents for agent list with positions
Frontend renders agent positions on 2D map from API data
CORS configured on FastAPI for frontend origin
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WebSocket bridge: real-time tick updates to frontend
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 2D map to update in real-time as agents move and act, without page refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend connects to ws://.../ws/dashboard WebSocket
Tick updates (agent positions, actions, chat) stream to frontend
2D map re-renders agent sprites on each update
Reconnects automatically on disconnect
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement macro tick (10min) + micro tick (30sec) engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need two tick layers: macro ticks for economic decisions every 10 minutes, and micro ticks for small activities every 30 seconds, so the world feels alive.
acceptanceCriteria: TickEngine has configurable macro_interval (default 600s) and micro_interval (default 30s)
Micro ticks trigger lightweight agent actions (move, think, chat)
Macro ticks trigger economic actions (trade, contract, reputation update)
Both tick types broadcast updates via WebSocket
Tests cover macro/micro tick sequencing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add time-of-day cycle to simulation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see agents follow a daily rhythm (morning→afternoon→evening→night) so the world feels alive.
acceptanceCriteria: World state includes current time_of_day (morning/afternoon/evening/night)
6 macro ticks = 1 full day cycle
Agent prompts include time-of-day context
Frontend displays current time-of-day indicator
Tests cover day cycle transitions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add agent position and movement system
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see agents move around the 2D map, visiting locations like the market, bank, and town square.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent model has x, y position fields
Predefined locations: market, bank, town_square, residential (with coordinates)
Agents move to relevant locations based on actions (trade→market, deposit→bank)
Micro ticks update agent positions with smooth interpolation data
Frontend renders agent movement on 2D map
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep positions simple: grid-based or point-based. AI Town has pathfinding but we can start with instant teleport to locations.

userStories.title: Configure and seed 5-10 diverse agents with LLM
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see diverse AI agents with distinct personalities living in the world.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed script creates 5-10 agents with varied personalities
Each agent has: name, bio, personality traits (risk_tolerance, greed, honesty), initial balance
Agents use DeepSeek/Qwen API for LLM decisions (configurable)
Agent prompts include personality, world state, time-of-day, and recent history
All agents successfully complete at least one tick cycle
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Agent personalities should create natural drama: one greedy trader, one cautious banker, one social butterfly, one con artist, etc. Use client/brain.py decision engine.

userStories.title: Build event timeline feed UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a real-time feed of agent events (trades, chats, conflicts) like a social media timeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline panel shows recent agent events chronologically
Events include: trades, chat messages, reputation changes, contract events
Each event shows agent name, action, and timestamp
New events appear at top with subtle animation
Timeline auto-scrolls but pauses on hover
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the 'drama feed' — the core engagement mechanic. Events should read like a story: 'Marco sold 50 wheat to Yuki for $500' or 'Sage filed a lawsuit against Marco for breach of contract'.

userStories.title: Run 24-hour simulation and capture demo footage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to run the simulation for 24 real hours (= 24 agent days) and capture compelling footage for a demo GIF/video.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation runs stably for 24+ hours without crashes
At least 3 interesting events occur (trade, conflict, reputation change)
Screenshot/recording captures agents moving, trading, and chatting
LLM API costs stay within $5 for the 24h run
No memory leaks (RSS stays under 500MB)
userStories.notes: This is the validation milestone. If the simulation produces boring results, revisit agent personalities and prompts before making the demo.
